evidence_quotes.quote: publishers reserve the privilege to sever the users' access to games they have rightfully purchased -- not rented, not borrowed, but purchased.

paragraphs.text: Being a citizen of Northwestern Russia, a European region, I find it concerning that publishers reserve the privilege to sever the users' access to games they have rightfully purchased -- not rented, not borrowed, but purchased. I believe users own the games they purchase, at the very least that should be how it is. From personal experience, I vastly prefer the game "Overwatch" over its sequel, "Overwatch 2", and it is upsetting that I now only hold access to the latter after several years of playing the original game.
